Handles run in a tight contiguous hex block, consistent with a single batch-drawn insulation fill pattern. There are no LINE, TEXT, or block-INSERT entities that would indicate a wall centerline/boundary, a dimension object, or a furniture symbol.
rationale.rule: A block whose entire content is uniform small closed polylines on a layer suffixed INSUL represents insulation fill/hatch-boundary geometry embedded in a floor-plan namespace; such homogeneous polygon sets should be scored with low wall_likelihood and empty wall_line_handles even though the layer sits under an X-평면도 (floor plan) prefix, because insulation fill cells are not themselves wall centerline/boundary entities.
rationale.counterfactual: If any of these entities were plain 2-point LINEs (or long thin closed polylines spanning a full room-length run) on a layer named 벽체/WALL rather than INSUL, or if the layer histogram showed a mix including wall-typical entities, wall_likelihood would rise sharply toward 1.0.
rationale.uncertainty: Without absolute vertex coordinates or the parent drawing's wall layer for cross-reference, it cannot be confirmed whether this insulation cell run traces an actual wall path in this drawing or is a standalone insulation swatch/legend; this projection provides no bbox and no coordinate data.
